Department Brief

Southview's front line, on duty since day one.

The Southview Police Department serves as the primary law enforcement agency operating within Southview. We exist for one reason: to keep the streets, and the story, running the way a real department would — with structure, accountability, and people who take the badge seriously.

Our core responsibilities cover public order and safety, upholding the law as it's written, and actively preventing, detecting, and investigating criminal activity across the city. Every division, every rank, and every case on file exists to support that mission.

SVPD isn't just a name tag in a Roblox game — it's a community built by people who wanted a department worth showing up for. Founded on April 14th, 2026, we're still early, which means there's real room to shape what this agency becomes.

What We Offer

Built for people who want to belong to something.

Whether you're brand new to roleplay law enforcement or you've worn a badge in other communities, here's what you're walking into.

01

Open Positions

We're actively growing. Multiple ranks and roles are open right now for anyone ready to put in the work.

02

A Welcoming Community

No gatekeeping, no cliques. New members are brought in and shown the ropes, not left to figure it out alone.

03

Multiple Divisions

Southview PD is built around specialized divisions, so there's a lane for different play styles and interests.

04

Constant Growth

We're a young department with a lot on the roadmap — new systems, ranks, and updates are always in motion.

Official Department Document

Chain of Command

Use of this document is to provide clarity regarding the chain of command and reporting structure within the Southview Police Department community.

Download PDF ← All Documents
On This Page
  1. Foundership
  2. Development
  3. Leadership
  4. Government
  5. High Command
  6. Headquarters
  7. High Ranks
  8. Middle Ranks
  9. Low Ranks
  10. Chain of Command Policy

👑Foundership

The Foundership serves as the highest authority within the community and maintains overall ownership, direction, and final decision-making authority.

Founder

  • Highest authority within the entire community.
  • Has final say on all matters.
  • Oversees all departments, leadership teams, and operations.

Co-Founder

  • Assists the Founder in managing the community.
  • May act on behalf of the Founder when necessary.
  • Supports community growth, administration, and strategic decisions.

Community Director

  • Oversees community-wide operations.
  • Assists Foundership with leadership and management responsibilities.
  • Helps coordinate major projects and departmental oversight.

🚧Development

Responsible for all development operations and technical improvements.

Head Developer

  • Leads the development team.
  • Oversees all development projects.
  • Reports directly to Foundership.

Senior Developer

  • Assists the Head Developer.
  • Supervises developers when required.
  • Helps manage ongoing projects.

Developer

  • Works on assigned development tasks.
  • Reports to Senior Developers and the Head Developer.

👥Leadership

Responsible for the day-to-day management of the community.

Community Manager

  • Oversees community operations.
  • Supervises community administration.
  • Assists Foundership with management duties.

Community Administration

  • Supports moderation and administrative operations.
  • Assists Community Managers.
  • Maintains community standards and procedures.

🏛Government

Government positions are obtained through elections and represent the community's roleplay government structure.

Governor

  • Highest elected government official.
  • Represents the interests of the community.
  • Oversees government initiatives and policies.

Lieutenant Governor

  • Assists the Governor.
  • Acts on behalf of the Governor when required.

Chief of Staff

  • Coordinates government operations.
  • Assists the Governor and Lieutenant Governor.

Secretary of Public Safety

  • Oversees public safety initiatives.
  • Works closely with Police Leadership.

⭐High Command

Responsible for the overall leadership of the Southview Police Department.

Chief of Police

  • Highest-ranking law enforcement officer.
  • Oversees all police operations and divisions.
  • Reports to Community Leadership and Government where applicable.

Deputy Chief of Police

  • Second-in-command of the police department.
  • Assists the Chief of Police.
  • Oversees departmental operations.

Assistant Chief of Police

  • Assists the Chief and Deputy Chief.
  • Helps manage divisions and specialized units.

🔷Headquarters

Responsible for division management and oversight.

Inspector

  • Leads their assigned division.
  • Responsible for division performance and operations.

Deputy Inspector

  • Assists the Inspector.
  • Acts in their absence when necessary.

Chief Superintendent

  • Assists divisional leadership.
  • Oversees senior personnel and operations.

Superintendent

  • Supports divisional command staff.
  • Assists with management and supervision.

🔶High Ranks

These ranks serve as senior command personnel responsible for departmental leadership, operational planning, supervision, and administration.

CommissionerDeputy CommissionerCommanderColonel

♦Middle Ranks

These ranks are responsible for supervising personnel, enforcing standards, and ensuring departmental efficiency.

MajorCaptainLieutenantSergeant

🔹Low Ranks

These ranks represent the operational backbone of the department and are responsible for carrying out day-to-day law enforcement duties.

CorporalConstablePolice OfficerPolice Recruit

📌Chain of Command Policy

All members are expected to follow the chain of command whenever possible. Members should:

  • Address concerns through their immediate supervisor first.
  • Respect higher-ranking personnel.
  • Follow lawful orders issued by superior officers.
  • Maintain professionalism at all times.

Failure to follow the chain of command may result in corrective action or disciplinary review.

Written ByTox
Approved ByJayden
Official Department Document

Code of Law

The official code of conduct, offence classifications, and disciplinary framework governing all Southview Police Department personnel.

Download PDF ← All Documents
On This Page
  1. Charge Classifications
  2. Professional Standards
  3. Corruption & Abuse of Power
  4. Chain of Command
  5. Confidential Information
  6. Equality & Respect
  7. Disciplinary Procedures
  8. Leadership Expectations
  9. Disciplinary Guidelines

ICharge Classifications

To ensure fairness and consistency, offences are divided into three categories: Minor, Major, and Capital. The severity of the offence will determine the disciplinary action taken.

Minor Offences (MIN)

Minor offences are less serious violations and will usually result in a warning, strike, or corrective action. Examples include:

  • Disrespect towards personnel.
  • Failure to follow simple instructions.
  • Uniform violations.
  • Unprofessional behaviour.
  • Minor misconduct.
  • Failure to maintain department standards.

Major Offences (MAJ)

Major offences are more serious and may result in investigations, suspension, demotion, or removal from a position. Examples include:

  • Abuse of authority.
  • False arrests.
  • Harassment of personnel.
  • Failure to follow the chain of command.
  • Negligence of duty.
  • Insubordination.
  • Repeated minor offences.
  • Misconduct while representing the department.

Capital Offences (CAP)

Capital offences are the most serious violations and may result in termination and blacklisting. Examples include:

  • Corruption.
  • Treason.
  • Leaking confidential information.
  • Exploiting or cheating.
  • Admin abuse.
  • Falsifying evidence.
  • Selling ranks or positions.
  • Recruiting members into competing communities.
  • Violating Roblox ToS, Discord ToS, or applicable laws.
  • Serious abuse of power.

IIProfessional Standards

All members of the Southview Police Department are expected to conduct themselves professionally at all times. Personnel should:

  • Treat others with respect.
  • Remain mature and professional.
  • Represent the department positively.
  • Follow department regulations and procedures.
  • Act with integrity and honesty.

Every member contributes to the reputation of the department and is expected to uphold these standards.

IIICorruption & Abuse of Power

Corruption and abuse of power have no place within the Southview Police Department. Examples include:

  • Favouring friends or specific individuals.
  • Unfair promotions.
  • Unfair disciplinary actions.
  • Misusing rank or authority.
  • Using permissions for personal gain.

Any member found guilty of corruption or abuse of power may face severe disciplinary action depending on the circumstances.

IVChain of Command

The chain of command exists to maintain order and accountability within the department. Personnel are expected to:

  • Follow lawful orders from supervisors.
  • Respect the department hierarchy.
  • Raise concerns through the appropriate channels whenever possible.

Failure to follow the chain of command may result in disciplinary action.

VConfidential Information

Certain information within the department is considered confidential and must not be shared without authorization. This includes:

  • Internal documents.
  • Personnel records.
  • Investigations.
  • Command discussions.
  • Department databases.

Sharing confidential information without permission may result in serious disciplinary action.

VIEquality & Respect

All members are entitled to fair and equal treatment. The department does not tolerate:

  • Harassment.
  • Bullying.
  • Discrimination.
  • Targeted disrespect.
  • Hostile behaviour towards personnel.

Every member should be given equal opportunities to learn, contribute, and progress within the department.

VIIDisciplinary Procedures

Disciplinary actions may include:

  1. Verbal Warning
  2. Written Warning
  3. Strike
  4. Suspension
  5. Demotion
  6. Termination
  7. Blacklist

The punishment issued will depend on the severity of the offence and any previous disciplinary history.

VIIILeadership Expectations

Members in leadership positions are expected to lead by example. Leaders should:

  • Maintain department standards.
  • Support and guide personnel.
  • Remain active in their duties.
  • Make fair and unbiased decisions.
  • Act professionally at all times.

Because of their position, leaders may be held to a higher standard of conduct than regular personnel.

IXDisciplinary Guidelines

The Southview Police Department aims to apply discipline fairly and consistently. Unless the offence is severe enough to justify immediate action, leadership should generally follow the disciplinary process below:

  1. Verbal Warning
  2. Written Warning
  3. Strike
  4. Final Warning / Fire Warning
  5. Suspension
  6. Demotion
  7. Termination
  8. Blacklist

Leadership may skip steps in cases involving Major or Capital offences depending on the severity of the violation.

All disciplinary actions should be supported by evidence and reviewed fairly before a final decision is made. The purpose of this process is to ensure consistency, fairness, and professionalism across the department.

Published BySVPD Command Staff
Written ByTox, Community Advisor
Approved ByJayden, Founder
Last Updated14 June 2026
Official Department Document

In-Game Regulations

Use of this document is strictly for all Southview community members. These regulations govern conduct during roleplay and community interaction.

Download PDF ← All Documents
On This Page
  1. Professional Conduct
  2. Community Behaviour
  3. Department Relations
  4. Abuse of Permissions
  5. Roleplay Standards
  6. Exploiting & Cheating
  7. Vehicle Regulations
  8. Staff Expectations
  9. Enforcement

IProfessional Conduct

All members are expected to conduct themselves professionally while representing Southview. Members must:

  • Treat others with respect.
  • Follow instructions from authorized staff.
  • Maintain maturity during roleplay and community interactions.
  • Represent Southview positively.
  • Avoid disruptive or toxic behaviour.

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action as outlined in the Southview Code of Law.

IICommunity Behaviour

Southview aims to provide a welcoming environment for all members. The following behaviour is prohibited:

  • Harassment.
  • Bullying.
  • Discrimination.
  • Excessive toxicity.
  • Targeted attacks against members.
  • Intentionally causing disruption within the community.

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action as outlined in the Southview Code of Law.

IIIDepartment Relations

All departments are expected to work together professionally and respectfully. Departments shall:

  • Respect the responsibilities of other departments.
  • Cooperate during operations and events.
  • Avoid unnecessary conflict.
  • Follow established procedures when working alongside other departments.

Inter-department disputes should be handled professionally and escalated through leadership if required. Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action as outlined in the Southview Code of Law.

IVAbuse of Permissions

Personnel may not abuse any permissions, tools, or authority granted to them. Examples include:

  • Admin abuse.
  • Abuse of department tools.
  • Unfair punishment of members.
  • Unauthorized use of commands.
  • Giving unfair advantages to individuals.

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action, suspension, removal from position, or termination depending on severity.

VRoleplay Standards

Members are expected to maintain realistic and professional roleplay standards. Personnel may not:

  • Randomly arrest players.
  • Abuse department equipment.
  • Ignore roleplay procedures.
  • Intentionally disrupt roleplay scenarios.
  • Fail to follow roleplay guidelines.

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action as outlined in the Southview Code of Law.

VIExploiting & Cheating

The use of exploits, cheats, scripts, or unfair advantages is strictly prohibited. This includes:

  • Third-party software.
  • Exploiting game bugs.
  • Bypassing game systems.
  • Assisting exploiters.

Violations may result in immediate termination and blacklist from Southview.

VIIVehicle Regulations

Personnel operating department vehicles must do so responsibly. Personnel must:

  • Drive safely.
  • Avoid reckless driving.
  • Use vehicles for authorized purposes.
  • Follow department vehicle procedures.

Failure to comply may result in loss of vehicle privileges, disciplinary action, or suspension.

VIIIStaff Expectations

Staff members are expected to lead by example and uphold community standards. Staff should:

  • Remain professional.
  • Act fairly and without bias.
  • Assist community members when needed.
  • Follow all community regulations.
  • Use permissions responsibly.

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action, removal from staff, or termination.

IXEnforcement

These regulations may be enforced by:

  • Department Leadership
  • Internal Affairs
  • Community Management
  • Ownership

Failure to comply with these regulations may result in:

  • Verbal Warning
  • Written Warning
  • Strike
  • Suspension
  • Demotion
  • Termination
  • Blacklist

The punishment issued will depend on the severity and frequency of the violation.

Written ByTox, Community Advisor
Approved ByJayden, Founder
Published14/06/2026
Official Department Document

Inspection Handbook

Use of this document is strictly for authorized South Police Department personnel only.

Download PDF ← All Documents 🔒 Internal Use — Authorized Personnel Only
On This Page
  1. Purpose
  2. Preparing an Inspection
  3. Announcement Format
  4. Uniform Standards
  5. Attending Personnel
  6. Parade Leader Responsibilities
  7. Inspecting Officer Responsibilities
  8. Inspection Procedure
  9. Evaluation Criteria
  10. Conclusion

Purpose

The purpose of inspections is to ensure that all South Police Department personnel maintain the highest standards of professionalism, discipline, and operational readiness. Inspections are conducted to verify that officers are wearing the correct uniform, carrying authorized equipment, following department regulations, and representing the department appropriately.

Inspections are not intended to embarrass personnel but to identify areas for improvement and maintain a consistent professional standard throughout the department.

Preparing an Inspection

Before an inspection begins, the Inspecting Officer or Parade Leader must ensure that the inspection area is prepared and free of obstructions.

An inspection announcement should be made several minutes in advance, giving personnel enough time to prepare. Officers are expected to arrive on time wearing the correct uniform and equipment.

Personnel who arrive late may be questioned regarding their absence and may be inspected separately at the discretion of the Inspecting Officer.

Announcement Format

Use the following format when announcing an inspection:

Event: Department Inspection
Status: Begins in 5 Minutes
Location: Parade Grounds
Attendance: Required Personnel
Uniform: Full Duty Uniform

Personnel should report using the following format:

Rank, Name, Position, Division, Ready for Inspection, Sir/Ma'am.

Uniform Standards

All officers are expected to wear the correct department-issued uniform and authorized equipment. The following are prohibited unless specifically authorized:

  • Incorrect uniforms
  • Unauthorized accessories
  • Missing duty equipment
  • Unprofessional avatars or appearances
  • Clothing that does not meet department regulations

Failure to comply may result in corrective action or a failed inspection.

Attending Personnel

All attending personnel will assemble outside the inspection area until instructed otherwise.

Once ordered to move, officers will enter the inspection grid in an orderly manner and occupy one designated marker. Officers must maintain equal spacing and remain standing at Attention.

Talking, unnecessary movement, jumping, or breaking formation is prohibited unless instructed by Command Staff.

When approached by the Inspecting Officer, personnel will greet them professionally and state:

Rank, Name, Position, Division, Ready for Inspection, Sir/Ma'am.

After being inspected, personnel will remain at Parade Rest until the inspection has concluded.

Parade Leader Responsibilities

The Parade Leader is responsible for organizing all attending personnel before and during the inspection. Their responsibilities include:

  • Forming personnel into the correct order.
  • Ensuring proper spacing and discipline.
  • Leading the formation onto the inspection grid.
  • Reporting readiness to the Inspecting Officer.
  • Maintaining professionalism throughout the event.

The Parade Leader should set an example for all personnel by demonstrating confidence, discipline, and leadership.

Inspecting Officer Responsibilities

The Inspecting Officer is responsible for conducting the inspection in a fair and professional manner. During the inspection, they may evaluate:

  • Uniform compliance.
  • Overall appearance.
  • Department equipment.
  • Professional conduct.
  • Knowledge of department procedures.
  • Bearing and discipline.
  • Communication skills.

The Inspecting Officer may ask department-related questions or provide corrective guidance where necessary.

Inspection Procedure

  1. Personnel gather at the designated assembly point.
  2. The Parade Leader forms all personnel into a Single File Line.
  3. The Inspecting Officer confirms readiness.
  4. The Parade Leader leads personnel onto the inspection grid.
  5. Officers occupy their assigned markers and stand at Attention.
  6. The Inspecting Officer inspects each officer individually.
  7. Officers introduce themselves using the approved reporting format.
  8. Once inspected, officers return to Parade Rest.
  9. After all inspections are complete, Command Staff provide feedback.
  10. The formation is formally dismissed.

Evaluation Criteria

Personnel may be evaluated on:

  • Correct uniform.
  • Professional appearance.
  • Authorized equipment.
  • Bearing and posture.
  • Knowledge of department policies.
  • Communication and confidence.
  • Ability to follow instructions.
  • Discipline.
  • Punctuality.
  • Overall professionalism.

Minor deficiencies may result in coaching, while repeated or serious issues may require further action.

Conclusion

Once every officer has been inspected, the Inspecting Officer will provide general feedback to the formation. Individual officers may receive additional comments or recommendations for improvement.

Outstanding performance should be recognized where appropriate, while any deficiencies should be corrected through coaching, additional training, or departmental guidance.

The inspection concludes when the formation has been formally dismissed by Command Staff.

Written Bylol_tox
Approved ByJayden
Version1.0
Last Updated17/06/2026
Legal

Privacy Policy

Last updated: 12 July 2026

← Back Home

What This Site Is

Southview Police Department (SVPD) is a fan-run Roblox roleplay community. This website is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by Roblox Corporation or Discord Inc. It exists to support the SVPD community and is run by volunteers.

Information We Collect

When you choose to sign in with your Roblox account, we receive and store the following information through Roblox's official sign-in system:

  • Your Roblox user ID, username, and display name.
  • Your Roblox avatar image.
  • Your rank within the SVPD Roblox group, if you are a member.

We do not receive your Roblox password, email address, payment information, or any data outside what Roblox's sign-in system explicitly shares.

Why We Collect It

This information is used only to:

  • Show you your own rank and name when you're signed in.
  • Determine what parts of the site you're permitted to view or edit, based on your position within the department.
  • Keep a record of who made changes to department records, for accountability.

How It's Stored

Your data is stored securely using Supabase, a third-party database provider, and is not sold, shared, or used for advertising. Session tokens expire automatically after 7 days.

Your Choices

You can choose not to sign in with Roblox and still browse all public pages of this site, including the Blacklist Registry and department documents. If you'd like your stored data removed, contact department leadership through our Discord.

Contact

Questions about this policy can be directed to SVPD leadership via our Discord server.

Legal

Terms of Service

Last updated: 12 July 2026

← Back Home

Acceptance of Terms

By using this website, including signing in with your Roblox account, you agree to these terms. If you don't agree, you can still browse the site's public pages without signing in.

Not Affiliated With Roblox or Discord

Southview Police Department is an independent, fan-run roleplay community. This site is not operated, sponsored, or endorsed by Roblox Corporation or Discord Inc. Use of Roblox sign-in is governed by Roblox's own Terms of Use in addition to these terms.

Account Access & Permissions

Your access level on this site is determined by your rank within the SVPD Roblox group at the time you sign in. Department leadership reserves the right to adjust access levels, remove content, or revoke access at their discretion, consistent with SVPD's Code of Law.

Conduct

Members using this site are expected to follow SVPD's Code of Law and In-Game Regulations. Misuse of edit permissions, including unauthorized changes to department records, may result in disciplinary action as outlined in those documents.

No Warranty

This site is provided as-is by volunteer community members. We make no guarantees about uptime, accuracy of records, or availability, and are not liable for any loss arising from use of the site.

Changes to These Terms

These terms may be updated as the site and department evolve. Continued use of the site after changes means you accept the updated terms.

Contact

Questions can be directed to SVPD leadership via our Discord server.

© 2026 Southview Police Department — Roblox Roleplay Community. Not affiliated with any real law enforcement agency.
